pi_items.source: Wireshark evidence_docs README.md (lines 227-234) confirms this file exists as official Wireshark sample capture
Filename is task-defined output; task.json specifies this as the expected filename

Wireshark official documentation and community resources confirm this filter correctly selects TCP SYN packets without ACK flag. Documented in Wireshark Display Filter guide and multiple Wireshark Q&A forums.
Task.json and setup_task.sh confirm this path is used consistently throughout
Wireshark evidence_docs README.md lines 40 and 69 confirm: '200722_tcp_anon.pcapng: 35 packets' from actual environment setup verification
Wireshark evidence_docs README.md line 40 shows: '/home/ga/Documents/captures/200722_tcp_anon.pcapng: 12800 bytes' from environment setup logs

pi_items.verified_value: Real network traffic - Official Wireshark SampleCaptures repository

Line 226 confirms: 'All data files are real network captures from the official Wireshark SampleCaptures wiki page.'

pi_items.verified_value: Netcat - string, file and characters (TCP mixed traffic)
pi_items.source: Wireshark SampleCaptures wiki page describes this file as containing Netcat client-server communication with string, file and character data transfers

privileged_info_summary: The source PCAP file '200722_tcp_anon.pcapng' is a real network capture (not synthetic) from Wireshark's official sample repository, containing 35 packets of TCP traffic totaling 12,800 bytes, demonstrating Netcat communication with string, file, and character transfers. The filter expression 'tcp.flags.syn == 1 && tcp.flags.ack == 0' is correct for selecting TCP SYN packets without the ACK flag, excluding SYN-ACK responses. The expected output path is '/home/ga/Documents/captures/syn_packets.pcapng' as specified in task configuration files.
